metadata.title: Why One Subagent Query Saved 48% of the Context Window

beats.title: Liam cold open — ClaudeComposerAsk
beats.narration_text: Jambo, Liam — in for Bear. Twenty-two percent remains? Can you explain it, Bear?

The grading-tool session has been running for an hour. Thirty percent context used. The teacher needs to investigate the late-submission policy. They ask Claude — in the same session — to read the policy docs. The session hits seventy-eight percent context. Twenty-two percent remains.

card.copy: Why one subagent query saved 48% of the context window

beats.new_visual_element: [TITLE] subagent saves 48% of the context window

beats.narration_text: The context window is the session's working memory. Every file Claude reads in the session stays there. The grading build: thirty percent. The policy doc, the LMS export, the meeting notes, three policy revisions: forty-eight percent more. Total: seventy-eight percent. The remaining twenty-two percent is barely enough for one student's feedback.

production_viz.label: context window fills: build 30% + research 48% = 78% used
production_viz.mechanic: a vertical context bar filling from the bottom: first 30% teal (grading build), then 48% crimson (research reads); a label '22% remains' at the top; a note 'barely enough for one feedback draft'

beats.narration_text: Here is the question. Adding a research task to a productive session should extend the session's usefulness. Here is the session where a ten-minute research task left only twenty-two percent of the context window for the remaining build work. Why?

card.copy: 10 minutes of research. 48% of the window gone. Why?

beats.narration_text: Every file Claude reads in a session stays in that session's context window. The research reads the policy doc — in the window. Reads the LMS export — in the window. Reads the meeting notes, three policy revisions, a section of the syllabus — all in the window. The session has no way to unread a file. The research work and the build work share the same space, and the space does not grow.

production_viz.label: same context window: build and research compete for the same space
production_viz.mechanic: a single context bar with crimson segments arriving one at a time — policy doc, LMS export, meeting notes, syllabus section — each segment added pushes the build context toward being evicted; a serif label 'one window, no unread'

beats.narration_text: A subagent is a Claude session that runs in its own isolated context window. The main session tells it what to read. The subagent reads everything in its own window. The subagent returns only a summary — three paragraphs, three hundred words. The main session receives the summary. The main session's context grows by three hundred words, not by every file the subagent read.

production_viz.label: subagent: its own window; main session gets only the summary
production_viz.mechanic: two boxes: left 'subagent context' filling with crimson policy-doc reads; right 'main session context' in teal, unchanged; an arrow labeled 'summary (300 words)' from left box to right box; the main session grows by only the arrow

beats.new_visual_element: [GRAPHIC] subagent context fills; main session receives only summary

beats.narration_text: The side-by-side. Without subagent: grading build at thirty percent, research reads push to seventy-eight percent, twenty-two percent remains. With subagent: grading build at thirty percent, subagent summary adds two percent, the window is at thirty-two percent. The build context is intact. The research is done.

production_viz.label: without subagent: 78% used; with subagent: 32% used
production_viz.mechanic: two context bars side by side: left 'without subagent' — 30% teal + 48% crimson; right 'with subagent' — 30% teal + 2% small teal summary; labels '22% remains' vs '68% remains'

beats.new_visual_element: [GRAPHIC] two context bars: 78% used vs 32% used

beats.narration_text: Here is where this bites in the classroom. The teacher's grading tool processes twenty-five submissions. Without subagent: Claude reads all twenty-five into the main session. The session degrades. The last five students get shallower analysis than the first five. With a pattern-analyzer subagent: all twenty-five are read in the subagent's window. The main session gets a structured pattern report. The session quality is uniform across all twenty-five.
beats.scene_description: a stack of 25 student submission papers next to a short summary document, editorial collage, desaturated
beats.image_prompt: B07, a thick stack of student submission papers on the left and a single short summary page on the right, the stack taller and messier, the summary page clean and small, both pinned to aged newsprint, editorial collage style, highly desaturated, cream and charcoal tones, no human faces
beats.new_visual_element: [STILL ai] stack of submissions vs single summary page

beats.narration_text: The heuristic: if the task reads more than the main session needs to see, it is a subagent task. Research tasks with many source docs. Pattern analysis across a corpus. Any work that generates a large intermediate read that can be compressed into a short summary. The subagent is not overhead. It is the design choice that makes long builds possible.

production_viz.label: the heuristic: reads more than session needs to see -> subagent
production_viz.mechanic: a two-row teal card drawing in: 'task reads many docs -> subagent'; 'task returns a summary -> subagent'; a serif label 'the subagent is the design, not the workaround'

beats.new_visual_element: [GRAPHIC] heuristic card: reads more than session needs

beats.narration_text: Research fills context. The subagent takes the fill. The main session keeps the build.

card.copy: Research fills context. The subagent takes the fill. The build stays clean.

beats.title: Liam verdict — ClaudeVerdictArtifact
beats.narration_text: Here's what the body just demonstrated. The heuristic: if the task reads more than the main session needs to see, it is a subagent task. Research tasks with many source docs. Pattern analysis across a corpus. Any work that generates a large intermediate read that can be compressed into a short summary. The subagent is not overhead. It is the design choice that makes long builds possible. Research fills context. The subagent takes the fill. The main session keeps the build.

beats.title: Your turn handoff — ClaudeComposerAsk
beats.narration_text: Take this prompt, run it on your own — pick any cancer type or clinical scenario you know about and ask how this mechanism applies there.

props.command: Explain how [Why One Subagent Query Saved 48% of the Context Window] applies to a specific cancer type or clinical case you're studying. What proteins are involved, what goes wrong at the molecular level, and what is the therapeutic or diagnostic significance?
